content: While four victims died on the spot, others succumbed to their injuries soon after the incident, the officer said.

content: At the all-India level, the monthly estimates are based on information collected from a total of 3,75,262 persons surveyed

title: Woman ‘killed, eaten’ by unidentified animal in The Nilgiris
content: The incident is likely to have occurred on April 11, but her remains were discovered on Wednesday; Hair samples from the scene have been collected to identify the animal species

title: Courts can't sit in judgement in matters of religious belief of denomination: TDB tells Supreme Court
content: A nine-judge Constitution Bench headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant was told by the TDB that the beliefs and practices of the community have to be judged by the subjective belief of the community and the court is bound to accept their belief

title: Final decision rests with Tamil Nadu people: DMDK’s Premallatha Vijayakant on talks about hung Assembly
content: The DMDK general secretary is confident about winning at Vriddhachalam constituency of Cuddalore district where she is contesting from, as part of the DMK-led alliance in the April 23 Assembly election in Tamil Nadu.

title: Women’s Reservation Bill and Delimitation 2026: Key takeaways
content: The proposed legislation seeks to expand the Lok Sabha seats from 543 to 850, addressing demographic shifts since the last adjustment in 1976, thus reshaping political power across the nation.

content: The case arises from a press conference addressed by Mr. Khera on April 4, 2026, in which he alleged that the Assam Chief Minister’s wife held three passports issued by different countries and levelled allegations of illegality against Mr. Sarma

content: The phones were recovered from Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana, and various districts of Andhra Pradesh, SP Vikrant Patil said

content: Amit Shah had assured party representatives, who met him in recent weeks, that the strength of the Lok Sabha would increase by 50%, to be distributed across States in their existing proportions — that is, a 50% increase in the number of Lok Sabha seats from each individual State

title: Nandyal Collector orders action against absent PHC doctors after surprise visit
content: Nandyal Collector Rajakumari Ganiya found two doctors and staff absent at a PHC in Mahanandi mandal and ordered disciplinary action

content: APSDMA and NRSC signed an MoU to map lightning hotspots, improve early warnings, and cut deaths from strikes in rural Andhra Pradesh

content: “Hoist black flags against the BJP, which is leading India into a dark period,” said Mr. Stalin in a video message

title: For 33% quota for women before next parliamentary polls, Lok Sabha seats to be increased to up to 850
content: The seats reserved for women in Lok Sabha and legislative Assemblies "shall be allotted by rotation to different constituencies in a state or Union territory," the Bill circulated among Lok Sabha members said

title: Centre mulls delimitation based on 2011 Census to fast-track Women’s Reservation Act
content: “Under the amendments that the government wants to bring in, the basis for the selection of seats will be the Census data of 2011. The number of seats in Parliament will increase to 816, out of which 273 will be reserved for women,” a source said

content: Why will the implementation be delayed by several years? When will the Census and delimitation exercise take place? Is there a timeline? Why are some parties demanding a sub-quota for women from Other Backward Classes?

content: Two senior leaders from the JD(U), Bijendra Prasad Yadav and Vijay Kumar Choudhary, also took oath as Ministers

content: Telangana Jagruti president opposes delimitation, cautions of large scale movement if State’s share is diluted

title: ACB takes Andhra Pradesh Endowments official K. Santhi into custody in corruption case
content: The ACB took Assistant Commissioner (Endowments) K. Santhi into two days’ police custody on Wednesday after a court order.

title: From veshtis to hoodies: How Tamil Nadu’s election fashion got a Gen Z makeover
content: Ahead of the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, political style is no longer top-down — from DMK’s Stalin to TVK’s Vijay, leaders, supporters, and fans are fuelling a shift to customised outfits, party merch, and social media-driven fashion

title: Four killed, 20 injured in gas cylinder explosion at Kummaravandla Palli in Kadiri, Sathya Sai district
content: Gas cylinder explosion was powerful enough to demolish two houses at Kummaravandla Palli village in Kadiri mandal of Sri Sathya Sai district in Andhra Pradesh

content: The plan to link women’s reservation in Parliament with delimitation has created a sharp political divide.

content: ‘Bangla Pokkho’, announced that a rally would be taken out in the evening of April 15 to celebrate 'Nabobarsho' (New Year) in the southern parts of the city, defying the EC's denial

title: Madras High Court stays trial in disproportionate assets case against T.N. Minister Anitha Radhakrishnan
content: Chief Justice’s Bench grants the interim stay pursuant to a plea by ED to transfer the case from Thoothukudi to Madurai

title: Himachal CM Sukhu announces ₹1,500 monthly aid for eligible women in Kinnaur
content: Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu says support will be provided under the Indira Gandhi Pyari Behna Sukh Samman Nidhi scheme; highlights border trade via Shipki La Pass and fiscal challenges in Himachal Pradesh

title: 'Xi will give me big, fat hug': Trump says he opened Hormuz for China ... 'and also the world'
content: President Trump claims China is pleased with his decision to permanently open the Strait of Hormuz, stating he's acting for their benefit and the world's. He asserts China has agreed to cease weapon shipments to Iran and anticipates a warm reception from President Xi during an upcoming visit, highlighting smart cooperation over conflict.

title: 'Hissa chori': Rahul accuses Centre of 'power grab' using delimitation
content: Rahul Gandhi accused the Centre of a power grab through a delimitation bill, stating the women's reservation bill was already passed in 2023. He asserted the Congress party unequivocally supports women's reservation and condemned the proposed increase in Lok Sabha seats as 'hissa chori' from marginalized communities and certain regions.

title: Vance lauds his Indian-origin in-laws, H1-B workers; stresses prioritising American identity first
content: US Vice President JD Vance emphasized that while immigrants enrich America, new citizens must prioritize their American identity. Speaking at a Turning Point USA event, Vance acknowledged the economic contributions of H-1B visa holders, including his Indian-origin in-laws, but warned against fraud and misuse.

title: 'Ghost' army ready: How Australia aims to tip the balance in Indo-Pacific
content: Australia is rapidly advancing its autonomous warfare capabilities with the Ghost Shark underwater drone and Ghost Bat aerial drone. These homegrown programs, moving from prototype to production, aim to bolster regional strategic balance and reduce risks to human personnel in high-threat environments.

title: Pakistan plans 2-hour daily power cuts as Iran war drives up energy costs
content: Pakistan is implementing two-hour evening power cuts due to soaring energy costs and Iran war-related disruptions. Reduced hydropower and reliance on expensive fossil fuels are straining the nation's finances. While urban areas face shorter outages, rural regions, particularly under MEPCO, are experiencing severe, prolonged blackouts, with gas supply to power plants also significantly impacted.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 28: Ranveer Singh film marches toward Rs. 1100 crore milestone
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is redefining box office history with every passing day. Directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun, the espionage thriller has become the first Hindi film ever to cross Rs. 1,000 crore in India net collections. On day 28, the film is chasing the Rs. 1,100 crore domestic milestone with a live India net of Rs. 1,096.19 crore and a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,727.93 crore.

title: Virat Kohli not in XI, star RCB pacer returns against LSG
content: RCB batter Virat Kohli was left out of RCB’s starting XI against Lucknow Super Giants due to an ankle injury but remained available as an Impact Player. Captain Rajat Patidar chose to bowl first, while Rishabh Pant stayed confident. RCB brought in Josh Hazlewood as both teams looked to gain momentum.

title: Perplexity AI Chief Communications Officer’s 'message' for Google: Web search is 'primitive’
content: Perplexity AI is challenging the status quo in online search, arguing that current technology is primitive and ripe for AI-driven innovation.  The company believes the real breakthrough lies in enhancing the 'read' function of the web, moving beyond simple link retrieval to deliver direct, objective-based answers.  This approach targets 'curious decision-makers' rather than relying on traditional ad models.

title: Bombay HC likely to give protection of personality rights to Kartik Aaryan
content: Bombay High Court indicated it would direct the removal of online content violating Kartik Aaryan's personality rights. The court will issue an order to address unauthorized commercial use of his name and image, including AI-generated videos and merchandise sales. This follows Aaryan's plea to protect his publicity and privacy rights.

title: ‘I might not be here’: Ben Stokes ‘lucky to be alive’ after freak injury scare
content: Ben Stokes described a training incident that broke his cheekbone as a "pretty nasty" but ultimately fortunate event, stating he feels "quite lucky" to be alive. The injury delayed his return to cricket, forcing a reassessment of his fitness plan. Stokes also dismissed speculation about his working relationship with Brendon McCullum, emphasising their shared goal for the team.

title: ‘Sindarov got lucky’: German No. 1 Keymer on ‘critical moment' | Exclusive
content: Exclusive: Javokhir Sindarov won the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament, earning a World Championship match against D Gukesh. Germany No. 1 Vincent Keymer praised his aggressive and fearless style of play. "It was easy for him to lose the first game," said Keymer as Uzbek prodigy sealed the title with a round to spare.

title: CBSE class 10 result 2026: Region-wise pass percentage out; Trivandrum, Vijaywada top the list
content: CBSE Class 10 results for 2026 are out. Trivandrum and Vijayawada achieved the highest pass rates. Southern regions showed strong performance. Delhi regions also performed well. Guwahati recorded the lowest pass percentage. The overall pass percentage saw a slight increase. Results indicate stable performance nationwide with minor regional variations.

title: Subtle signs of organ stress most people ignore and how to prevent damage
content: Your body whispers warnings before it screams in pain. Subtle signs like persistent fatigue, brain fog, skin changes, and minor discomforts often signal internal strain. Experts urge paying attention to these early indicators, as they can prevent serious organ damage. Lifestyle adjustments and regular check-ups are key to proactive health.

title: ‘End of an Era’: Viktor Axelsen retires at 32, Lakshya Sen pays emotional tribute
content: Denmark's two-time Olympic champion Viktor Axelsen, 32, has retired due to persistent back issues, ending a dominant era in badminton. His career, marked by two Olympic golds and two World Championship titles, redefined the sport's physical standards. Axelsen's influence extended globally, particularly in China, where he connected with fans in Mandarin.

title: First time in 7 yrs! India gets 4m barrels of crude from Iran just ahead of Trump waiver expiry
content: The very large crude carrier Jaya, fully loaded with Iranian oil, is currently unloading its cargo at Paradip on India’s eastern coast. India is looking to quickly secure supplies ahead of a deadline set by the Donald Trump administration that expires over the weekend.

title: NCA feels more like a 'Hospital'... players are fearful of going: Munaf Patel
content: Former India pacer Munaf Patel has voiced strong concerns about India's cricketing ecosystem, urging reforms at the National Cricket Academy (NCA) and domestic structure. He criticized the NCA's focus on rehabilitation over holistic development and highlighted the lack of a strong feeder pathway, advocating for strengthened grassroots infrastructure.

title: CBSE Class 10th result 2026 declared: 93.70% students pass; southern states dominate
content: The CBSE has declared the Class 10 board results for 2026 on April 15, recording a pass percentage of 93.70%, a slight rise from last year. Over 24.7 lakh students appeared across India and abroad. Girls again outperformed boys, while southern regions and Kendriya Vidyalayas led institutional performance in a largely stable academic outcome.

title: Z security & ED raids: AAP-Raghav Chadha faceoff turns ugly; BJP piles on
content: The Aam Aadmi Party's rift with MP Raghav Chadha has widened, with leaders accusing him of betrayal and linking ED raids on Ashok Mittal to his alleged collusion with the BJP. Chadha's removal as deputy leader and subsequent Z-category security have fueled internal dissent and public accusations.

title: Meet Yvon Chouinard: The billionaire who gave away his $3 billion company to save the planet
content: Patagonia founder Yvon Chouinard made a groundbreaking move, gifting his $3 billion company to environmental causes.  Instead of personal profit, the company's earnings now fund climate action and conservation. This unique structure, separating control from financial benefit, ensures Patagonia's profits directly support the planet's health, sparking global debate on purpose-driven business.

title: Court asks filmmaker Rajiv Rai to refrain from talking about 'Dhurandhar 2'
content: Filmmaker Rajeev Rai has assured the Delhi High Court he will not speak to the media about the 'Dhurandhar 2' song dispute during ongoing mediation. Trimurti Films alleges unauthorized use of their song. The court emphasized restraint to aid mediation, noting Rai's public remarks were causing prejudice.

title: 'Very serious': UN watchdog flags sharp rise in North Korea's nuclear activity
content: North Korea's nuclear weapons production capability is seeing a "very serious increase," according to the IAEA chief. Heightened activity at the Yongbyon complex, including its reactor and reprocessing unit, signals a significant expansion. New uranium enrichment facilities are also under construction, boosting production capacity. This development raises global concerns about regional security and nuclear proliferation.

title: Stalin vs Centre: Why is Tamil Nadu protesting the delimitation bill
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin announced a state-wide black flag protest against the Centre's proposed delimitation exercise. He warned of severe political consequences if concerns about reducing southern states' political weight are ignored. Stalin described the move as a 'grave danger' and a 'historic injustice' that could widen the North-South divide and undermine federalism.

title: Drama in PSL: Sikandar Raza fires back at Imad Wasim over 'money' remark
content: The comments date back to last year, when Imad spoke about Raza’s late arrival for the PSL 10 final at Gaddafi Stadium in Lahore. Raza had taken a partial leave from the Qalandars squad to play a one-off Test for Zimbabwe against England in Nottingham.

title: CBSE Class 10th result 2026 declared via DigiLocker and UMANG app
content: CBSE Class 10 results 2026 were declared at 4 pm on April 15, with students accessing marksheets via DigiLocker, UMANG, official websites, SMS, and IVRS. DigiLocker and UMANG serve as primary platforms, ensuring smooth access during high traffic. Students need credentials or APAAR-linked accounts to log in. Backup options like SMS and IVRS provide additional support. Digital marksheets include subject-wise scores and certification details, ensuring reliable and convenient result access across multiple channels.

title: Did China secretly help Iran track US bases? The 'spy satellite' in question
content: Iran reportedly used a Chinese-built spy satellite, TEE-01B, to monitor US military bases in the Middle East during recent conflicts. Leaked documents suggest the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps utilized the satellite for targeting and assessing strike success. China denies providing military support, calling the accusations fabricated.

title: Bombay high court dismisses Abu Salem’s premature remission plea
content: Bombay High Court dismissed Abu Salem's plea for release, deeming it premature. The court cited a Supreme Court order stating his 25-year sentence, including remission, would conclude in 2030, not now. Salem had claimed he had already served the maximum term agreed upon during his extradition.

title: Ecstasy decoded: How a ‘party pill’ turned fatal at Mumbai concert
content: Two MBA students' deaths at a Mumbai concert highlight ecstasy's dangers. Known as Molly or E, this party drug, often in colourful pills, can cause fatal overheating and organ strain. Unregulated potency and adulterants, especially when combined with dancing and alcohol, amplify risks, making timely recognition of symptoms crucial.

title: 'Bumrah is a star, but…': Munaf Patel drops truth bomb on Indian fast bowlers
content: Munaf Patel called Jasprit Bumrah a superstar but said his popularity still lags behind Virat Kohli’s. He also flagged India’s fast-bowling gap, noting 140+ kmph pacers get priority while others are overlooked, and stressed the need for better scientific training to develop genuine pace bowlers.

title: 'Secretly switched planes': What led Iran delegation to reroute after failed US talks in Pakistan
content: An Iranian delegation reportedly received direct threats and credible intelligence of a potential attack on their aircraft en route to Pakistan for talks with the US. This led to a diversion of their return journey to Mashhad, with the team then traveling to Tehran by land.

title: Bulldozer action in Amravati: House of Ayan Ahmed Tanveer demolished after abuse videos surface
content: Authorities in Amravati have launched a crackdown, including bulldozer action, against the residence of a 19-year-old accused in a shocking cyber-enabled sexual exploitation and blackmail case involving minors. The accused allegedly lured girls via social media, recorded objectionable content without consent, and circulated it online. Police have arrested the suspect and are investigating a wider network.

title: "Politically Motivated, Will Fight Women's Quota Bill": M Kharge After Opposition Meet
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge said: "We are in support of the Women's Reservation Bill but have objection to the government's approach".

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: US Denies Agreeing To Extend Iran Ceasefire: Report
content: US-Iran War Live: The Iran war began when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28. Iran responded with its own strikes on Israel and Gulf states that host US bases.

title: MK Stalin Calls For Black Flag Protest Across Tamil Nadu Over Delimitation
content: Stalin has urged every household in Tamil Nadu to participate in the protest, framing the delimitation issue as a fight to protect the state's political rights.

title: Video: Omar Abdullah Refuses To Cut 'Tricolour' Ribbon, Earns Praise
content: Chief Minister Omar Abdullah is seen walking towards the ribbon stretched across and then stopping short as he notices the national flag colours, shows a video.

content: The fresh bout of negotiations comes amid evolving tariff rules in the US, with officials indicating that clarity on Washington's trade stance remains crucial before any final deal.

title: Iran Warns US Blockade Threatens Ceasefire, Signals Red Sea Retaliation
content: Iran's military has also threatened to block trade through the Red Sea, along with the Gulf and Sea of Oman, if the US naval blockade continues.

content: Bengal with its duality - 38% women in Parliament versus 18% in the state contest - captures the core tension of the Bill better than any other example.

content: The ruling DMK has fielded Raja Anbazhagan, who is confident of retaining the seat with an increased margin.

title: Alleged Casteist Slurs, Loan App, And The Death Of A Dalit Student In Kerala
content: Nithin Raj, from a daily wage labourer's family in Thiruvananthapuram, had secured a BDS seat at Anjarakandy Dental College in Kannur. On the afternoon of April 10, he fell from the fifth floor of his college building and died.

title: "Ceasefire Must Start Now": Ex-Lebanese Minister Amid Deepening Crisis
content: Former Lebanese Health Minister Firass Abiad calls for immediate ceasefire amid healthcare collapse and rising civilian casualties.

title: Oil Tanker Blacklisted By US Crossed Hormuz With Its Tracker On, Claims Iran
content: The US has said it has begun what it says is ablockadeof any and all ships originating in Iranian ports and would interdict every vessel that has paid a toll to Iran.

title: Too Many Tigers, Jungle Too Small: Human-Animal Conflict In Land Of Mowgli
content: Madhya Pradesh's tiger population has surged from 785 in 2022 to an estimated 1,000 today. But the forests they depend on have not expanded with them.

content: For several days after the elderly woman was taken into custody, the family had no information about her whereabouts.

title: The legacy of Jackie Robinson, baseball's greatest agent of change
